behaviors.description: Reject invalid signature and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "signature does not match its body... body is not forwarded" requires checking handoff between verifier and queue publisher, not just hash math in isolation (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA is not required: can run in-process with wired handler + queue fake/sp y. Rejected higher level: no phrase requires real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local module wiring, and no AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire HTTP handler, signature verifier, and queue publisher in-process. Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256`; assert 401 and assert publisher is never called.

behaviors.description: Persist to queue before returning 200 ACK.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned" is an ordering contract across handler and queue modules (MOCK-MISS). Integration is enough with real queue module against local persistence (DB/filesystem) and response capture. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real external HTTP/network conditions; ordering can be caught in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real persistent-queue module (local store). Instrument/observe call order or durable write completion marker; assert write completes before 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing... to finish" is module interaction between request path and async worker trigger (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network requirement if downstream is a controlled delayed module in-process. Rejected System: no phrase requiring real subprocess/real network timing artifacts.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor where processor blocks on a latch. Send valid event; assert response returns before latch release and event is queued for later processing.

behaviors.description: Respond within 3 seconds under slow concurrent downstream load.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "within 3 seconds wall-clock time... slow and concurrent" requires real timing/concurrency behavior under HTTP serving load (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). In-process mocks can hide event-loop starvation, socket backlog, and scheduling effects. Rejected lower Integration: behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock and concurrent request handling characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Start real Node HTTP server process with actual handler/queue/worker wiring. Create concurrent load with slow downstream processing of prior events; measure end-to-end response latency for new requests and assert p95 or max within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not enqueued twice.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time" is state handoff/idempotency across request handling and queue persistence layers (MOCK-MISS). No OS/network/subprocess dependency is required to reproduce logical duplicate suppression. Rejected System: no behavior text demanding real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Using handler + real dedupe/queue modules with local persistence, submit same event ID twice; assert first write succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: Return 503 + Retry-After when queue is full.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header" tests mapping of queue-state signal to HTTP response contract across modules (MOCK-MISS). Integration is sufficient; no real external infra needed. Rejected System: no phrase requiring real network/OS behavior to expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue module configured to emit/return full condition. Send valid request; assert status 503 and `Retry-After` header present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit" targets queue component behavior with persistence and capacity accounting across internal modules. Per level defs, local DB/filesystem-backed queue fits Integration. Rejected System: no explicit requirement for subprocess/real external API/network; overflow logic is testable with local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with small configured capacity on local store, enqueue to limit, then enqueue one extra; assert overflow is rejected and stored count never exceeds configured limit.
